serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Nama Chocolate Drink is approximately 200 g (one cup), as typically consumed as a hot or cold cocoa-based beverage in a Singapore hawker or café setting.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — M-62 Nama Chocolate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for M-62 Nama Chocolate (cr_id G00011011). All per-100g values are as reported by HPB with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — cocoa/chocolate-based beverages, sweetened
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'M-62 Nama Chocolate' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sweetened cocoa/chocolate-based beverage (GI ~50–60, median 55) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Per standard GI classification (University of Sydney / WHO-FAO 1998), GI 55 falls in the LOW category (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (55 × 10.36 × 2) / 100 = 11.4 g. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with negligible potassium (5 mg/100 g) and zero dietary fibre; sodium is very low (3 mg/100 g) but does not offset the sugar-driven cardiovascular risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this beverage due to its high free-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) and negligible potassium, which together contribute to an unfavourable dietary pattern associated with elevated blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; choose unsweetened cocoa preparations instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre and zero protein results in rapid glucose absorption; GI of 55 (low boundary) with a meaningful glycaemic load of approximately 11.4 per standard 200 g serving represents a significant postprandial glucose challenge.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this sweetened cocoa drink because its high free-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g), absence of dietary fibre, and GI of 55 will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; unsweetened cocoa with a low-GI milk alternative is a preferable option.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with negligible fibre, potassium, and micronutrients; saturated fat is reported as zero and sodium is very low, but the sugar-dominant profile contributes to dyslipidaemia and metabolic risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, this beverage's high free-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with zero fibre and negligible cardioprotective nutrients (potassium 5 mg, calcium 1 mg per 100 g) makes it an unfavourable choice; the American Heart Association recommends limiting added-sugar beverages to reduce cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with zero fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; the diluted cocoa preparation delivers insufficient flavonoids to provide meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened cocoa drink as its high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) promotes inflammatory pathways, and the diluted preparation does not deliver sufficient cocoa flavonoids to offset this effect; unsweetened high-cocoa-content (>70%) preparations are preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nama Chocolate Drink (M-62) is a sweetened cocoa-based beverage listed in the Singapore HPB Foodo database under the code M-62. The drink is prepared by dissolving cocoa or chocolate powder with sugar in water or a dilute base, resulting in a lightly coloured, mildly chocolatey liquid with a sweet taste and thin, watery texture. It is not a dense ganache-style 'nama chocolate' confection; rather, it is a diluted, sugar-forward cocoa drink typical of hawker-centre or café beverage menus in Singapore. Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and 0 g protein and dietary fibre. Sodium is very low at 3 mg/100 g. The high free-sugar content with zero fibre and zero protein means rapid glucose absorption, making this beverage unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low category per University of Sydney / WHO-FAO classification: low ≤55) based on analogous sweetened cocoa beverages, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 11.4 per standard 200 g serving. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable. It is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner as a sweet accompaniment in Singapore food culture.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011011) with a reported confidence of 0.95 and review_status 'reviewed'. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI sub-component only) but the overall record confidence is high given the exact HPB match for all macros and micronutrients.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo (G00011011); this is a diluted cocoa-based beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients at detectable levels — confirmed zero per approved source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; the beverage contains no fibre-contributing ingredients — confirmed zero per approved source. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; total fat is only 0.25 g/100 g with no saturated fat reported — confirmed zero per approved source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — confirmed zero per approved source. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — confirmed zero per approved source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo for this entry; no approved secondary source provides these values for this specific preparation. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables for sweetened cocoa beverages; per the University of Sydney / WHO-FAO 1998 classification, GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the LOW category (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70); gi_category corrected to 'low' from previous draft's 'medium'. Direct GI measurement for M-62 Nama Chocolate is not available. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' and food_subgroup updated to 'Cocoa and chocolate-based beverages' based on the nutritional profile (42 kcal/100 g, 10.36 g carbohydrate, 0 g protein, 0.25 g fat) consistent with a diluted beverage rather than a solid mixed dish.
